programme.title: Balance of Power
programme.desc: Focusing on politics and policies on Capitol Hill, in President Trump's administration, the federal agencies and the courts.

programme.title: Bloomberg Businessweek
programme.desc: Carol Massar, David Gura. Bloomberg Television takes you behind the scenes of our award winning magazine "Bloomberg BusinessWeek"; Carol Massar and David Gura talk with the writers who break news on a weekly basis and get a lighter touch from the editor who oversees the Etc. section.

programme.title: How Chinese Brands Are Going Global
programme.desc: From Pop Mart's Labubus to Shein, Temu and Luckin, Chinese brands have become global consumer favorites and cultural ambassadors. With much of the credit going to TikTok, shoppers are hungry for more.

programme.title: Tesla's Dangerous Doors
programme.desc: A number of fatal car crashes involving Tesla vehicles has prompted increased scrutiny of its electric doors, triggering lawsuits over whether the design can leave passengers trapped.

programme.title: The Hospitals That Won't Let Patients Go Home
programme.desc: The International Finance Corp., an arm of the World Bank, spends millions of dollars of taxpayer money to build up private-sector services in the developing world. But a Bloomberg investigation reveals that several private hospital companies funded.

programme.title: The Hidden Cost of South Sudan's Oil Boom
programme.desc: South Sudan relies on oil for almost all of its revenue. But some communities living near the oil fields allege polluted water is causing birth defects and miscarriages, raising questions over health and responsibility.

programme.title: Inside The Deaths That Rocked India's Relations With The West
programme.desc: This is the story of how two alleged murder plots involving Sikh activists brought India, the US and Canada to a diplomatic crisis.

programme.title: How China Is Building an Army of Hackers
programme.desc: China and the US actively engage in cyber espionage for strategic advantage. Leaked files now suggest how rapidly Beijing is catching up in preparation for any future conflict.

programme.title: A Dangerous Corner of the Global Shipping Industry
programme.desc: The sinking of the Gulf Livestock 1 was the worst disaster in the history of the live-export trade, an industry made up of about 150 ships with a market value totaling $20 billion to $30 billion.

programme.title: The Trading Guru and His Money-Losing Disciples
programme.desc: IM Academy was a global phenomenon, claiming it could make anyone a top trader. Its founder, Chris Terry, a self-made trader turned multimillionaire who flaunted luxury cars, private jets and apartments across continents, said IM was more than.

programme.title: China's Controversial $167 Billion Mega-Dam
programme.desc: China's Controversial $167 Billion Mega-Dam

programme.title: Who Will Fill Jamie Dimon's Shoes?
programme.desc: Who Will Fill Jamie Dimon's Shoes?

programme.title: The Rise of America's ICE Towns
programme.desc: Small towns are at the center of President Donald Trump's mass deportation campaign, spearheaded by ICE; in one rural New Mexico county, officials are grappling with what it means to be an ICE town.

programme.title: Bloomberg Primer
programme.desc: Humanoid robots that use AI are going mainstream, but they're far from proven. With billions of dollars invested, can they deliver real-world value, or will they fall short of the hype?

programme.title: Bloomberg Primer
programme.desc: Chinese companies dominate the EV battery supply chain and are expanding rapidly outside their home market. Meanwhile, competitors are looking to new chemistries to leapfrog the leading tech.

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Bioacoustics technology and AI are revealing how animals communicate, and may soon allow humans to talk to them.

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: With the promise of unimaginable computing power, a global race for quantum supremacy is raging.

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Human survival depends on water, yet global reserves are rapidly shrinking; it's now replaced oil as the most likely cause of global conflict; discussing whether technology can help secure humanity's future water needs.

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Pain is a fact of life; for some, it is excruciating and inescapable; but amid the continuing fallout of the opioid crisis, the development of new technologies aims to limit and even eliminate pain.

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Soon, another billion people will be online, mostly from developing nations; ways new users will embrace this technology to transform their lives; its meaning for the rest of the world.

programme.title: Leaders with Francine Lacqua: Alexander Stubb, President of Finland
programme.desc: Francine Lacqua visits Finland, where the threat from neighboring Russia looms large; she speaks to President Alexander Stubb about Finnish resilience and how his country is preparing for an uncertain future.

programme.title: LEADERS With Francine Lacqua
programme.desc: Francine Lacqua meets Ray Dalio on OceanX in the port of Nice for the UN Ocean Conference. They speak about radical transparency, building and leaving one of the largest hedge funds and what's next.

programme.title: LEADERS With Francine Lacqua
programme.desc: Francine Lacqua talks with Franklin Templeton CEO Jenny Johnson about how to steer a $1.6 trillion business amid the rise of AI, shifting politics and booming private markets.

programme.title: LEADERS With Francine Lacqua
programme.desc: Former U.K. Prime Minister Theresa May speaks with Francine Lacqua about Vladimir Putin and her approach to leadership in a world seeing a rise in strongmen.

programme.title: LEADERS With Francine Lacqua
programme.desc: The anchor sits down with the decision-makers shaping our future to explore the personal stories behind their public personas.

programme.title: LEADERS With Francine Lacqua
programme.desc: What drives the world's most influential minds? In Leaders with Francine Lacqua, we sit down with the decision-makers shaping our future to explore the personal stories behind their public personas.

programme.title: Bloomberg Primer
programme.desc: Scientists are developing more powerful magnets to enable clean energy sources like fusion; China's dominance of the supply chain for rare earth magnets threatens their global availability.

programme.title: Bloomberg Primer
programme.desc: Exploring the world of biocomputing; scientists are laying the foundation for a field that may blur the lines between the biological and synthetic.

programme.title: Bloomberg Primer
programme.desc: Geothermal has been the underdog of the energy industry, but that may be about to change as new startups use fracking technologies to expand potential geothermal sites.

programme.title: Bloomberg Primer
programme.desc: Scientists and entrepreneurs are testing radical ideas like solar radiation management and ocean fertilization; they are finding funding for them.

programme.title: Bloomberg Primer
programme.desc: The idea of flying cars has largely remained science fiction, until now; a new generation of electrified aircraft is attracting the attention of investors and venture capitalists around the world.

programme.title: Bloomberg Primer
programme.desc: Governments and entrepreneurs are racing to commercialize the moon; these efforts could unlock a new, deep-space economy; but to return and stay will require the highest levels of innovation.

programme.title: Bloomberg Primer
programme.desc: Humanoid robots that use AI are going mainstream, but they're far from proven. With billions of dollars invested, can they deliver real-world value, or will they fall short of the hype?

programme.title: Bloomberg Primer
programme.desc: Chinese companies dominate the EV battery supply chain and are expanding rapidly outside their home market. Meanwhile, competitors are looking to new chemistries to leapfrog the leading tech.

programme.title: The Circuit
programme.desc: Emily Chang meets Reese Witherspoon and the Hello Sunshine executive team to discuss the company's founding mission and what's next for Hollywood.

programme.title: The Circuit With Emily Chang
programme.desc: Emily Chang meets Disney Entertainment Co-Chair Dana Walden for an inside look at the entertainment giant's global streaming and TV ambitions.

programme.title: The Circuit With Emily Chang
programme.desc: Emily Chang meets California's First Couple, Governor Gavin Newsom and Jennifer Siebel Newsom, visiting their home in Marin County and offices in Sacramento to see how they work together.

programme.title: China's Controversial $167 Billion Mega-Dam
programme.desc: China's Controversial $167 Billion Mega-Dam

programme.title: Tesla's Dangerous Doors
programme.desc: A number of fatal car crashes involving Tesla vehicles has prompted increased scrutiny of its electric doors, triggering lawsuits over whether the design can leave passengers trapped.

programme.title: A Dangerous Corner of the Global Shipping Industry
programme.desc: The sinking of the Gulf Livestock 1 was the worst disaster in the history of the live-export trade, an industry made up of about 150 ships with a market value totaling $20 billion to $30 billion.

programme.title: The Hospitals That Won't Let Patients Go Home
programme.desc: The International Finance Corp., an arm of the World Bank, spends millions of dollars of taxpayer money to build up private-sector services in the developing world. But a Bloomberg investigation reveals that several private hospital companies funded.

programme.title: The Hidden Cost of South Sudan's Oil Boom
programme.desc: South Sudan relies on oil for almost all of its revenue. But some communities living near the oil fields allege polluted water is causing birth defects and miscarriages, raising questions over health and responsibility.

programme.title: The Rise of America's ICE Towns
programme.desc: Small towns are at the center of President Donald Trump's mass deportation campaign, spearheaded by ICE; in one rural New Mexico county, officials are grappling with what it means to be an ICE town.

programme.title: The Deal With Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly
programme.desc: Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly talk with Emmanuel Acho about his reinvention from NFL linebacker to media leader, his show "Uncomfortable Conversations" and his new effort, Speakeasy.

programme.title: The Deal With Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly
programme.desc: Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly talk with USC Annenberg School for Communication and Journalism Dean Willow Bay about her investment in Angel City Football Club and her tenure on NBA Inside Stuff.

programme.title: The Deal With Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly
programme.desc: Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly speak with Steve Young about his journey from NFL stardom to managing a $10 billion fund, discussing the challenges and lessons learned along the way.

programme.title: The Deal With Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly
programme.desc: Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly sit with Erika Ayers Badan, former chief executive of Barstool Sports and current CEO of Food52, to discuss navigating turnarounds and how setbacks can fuel growth.
